skill.name: pennylane
skill.description: Guides PennyLane quantum computing work, including quantum circuits, hybrid quantum machine learning, VQE, QAOA, optimization, and backend selection.
skill.summary: Build and optimize PennyLane quantum circuits, quantum ML models, and chemistry workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command execution, weak crypto, filesystem, network, and credential patterns, but review found the skill is documentation-only markdown. Most command and weak-crypto hits are false positives from fenced examples and quantum terminology; the remaining caution is that examples mention package installation, provider tokens, hardware APIs, and external links.

medium_findings.title: Provider Credential Examples Need User Caution
medium_findings.description: The hardware backend documentation includes placeholder provider tokens and API keys for IBM Quantum and IonQ. These are not real secrets and no exfiltration code was found, but users could mishandle real credentials if they paste them into shared code or prompts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines clearly show credential placeholders in legitimate hardware examples. Confidence is below very high because the files do not access environment variables or transmit secrets.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Includes Install Commands
medium_findings.description: The external command findings are markdown installation examples for PennyLane and optional plugins, not executable skill code. They still ask users to install packages, so publication should warn users to review dependencies and use trusted environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are fenced bash snippets and package names, with no script hook or automatic execution path. The remaining risk is user-driven installation from documentation.

low_findings.title: External Links Are Informational
low_findings.description: The network findings are documentation links to PennyLane resources, GitHub, the community forum, and a K-Dense promotional URL. No code was found that performs network requests or sends local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to plain markdown URLs. No fetch, curl, webhook, telemetry, or data upload behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Hits Are Quantum Terminology
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography findings map to quantum computing terms and optimizer names such as Hadamard, gradient descent, and related PennyLane concepts. No cryptographic hashing, encryption, or password handling implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged context is quantum circuit and optimization documentation, not cryptographic code. The reviewed snippets do not implement weak algorithms like MD5 or SHA-1.

low_findings.title: Filesystem and Reconnaissance Hits Are Benign Text
low_findings.description: The filesystem and reconnaissance patterns are false positives from Python list append calls, device caching advice, and section text. No file reads, file writes, directory traversal, system probing, or local data collection was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain numerical list operations and best-practice prose. No Node.js fs API, shell reconnaissance command, or filesystem access pattern appears in context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Placeholders in Hardware Backend Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples show where hardware provider tokens or API keys would be supplied. The placeholders are not secrets, but users should replace them only through secure local configuration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is a real credential-handling topic, but there is no evidence of secret harvesting or network exfiltration in the skill files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Installation Commands in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains bash snippets that tell users to install PennyLane and hardware plugins. These commands are legitimate for the topic, but users should review package sources before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are static documentation snippets and are not executed by the skill. They still represent user-action risk because package installation changes the local environment.

content.user_title: Build Quantum ML Workflows with PennyLane
content.value_statement: Quantum application development requires careful circuit, optimizer, and backend choices. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused PennyLane guidance for simulations, hybrid models, and hardware workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains PennyLane installation and plugin choices for simulators and quantum hardware.
Guides QNode construction with gates, measurements, templates, and circuit inspection.
Supports hybrid quantum-classical model design with PyTorch, JAX, and TensorFlow concepts.
Covers VQE, molecular Hamiltonians, UCCSD, and quantum chemistry workflows.
Compares optimizers, gradient methods, device backends, and performance options.
Provides reference guidance for QAOA, noise models, transforms, and Catalyst compilation.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not execute PennyLane code by itself.
Hardware access still requires valid provider accounts, plugins, credentials, and quota.
Examples may need updates for installed PennyLane and plugin versions.
It does not replace validation on simulators before costly hardware runs.

use_cases.title: Prototype Quantum Circuits
use_cases.description: Design QNodes, choose measurements, inspect circuit structure, and test circuits on local simulators.

use_cases.title: Train Hybrid Models
use_cases.description: Plan quantum neural networks, data encodings, optimizers, and framework integration for experimental QML models.

use_cases.title: Run Chemistry Experiments
use_cases.description: Set up molecular Hamiltonians, VQE circuits, ansatz choices, and optimization checks for chemistry studies.

prompt_templates.title: Create a First QNode
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a simple PennyLane QNode for two qubits. Explain the device, gates, measurement, and how to run it on a simulator.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Optimizer
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a variational PennyLane circuit with noisy gradients. Compare suitable optimizers and gradient methods, then recommend a training plan.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a VQE Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a PennyLane VQE workflow for a small molecule. Include Hamiltonian setup, ansatz choice, initialization, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Port to Hardware
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my PennyLane simulator workflow and plan the changes needed to run it on a hardware backend with safe credential handling.

output_examples.input: User asks how to start a two-qubit PennyLane experiment.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step plan that selects a local simulator, defines a QNode, chooses simple rotation gates, and explains the expected measurement result.

output_examples.input: User asks why a variational model is not training well.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering initialization, learning rate, gradient method, barren plateaus, circuit depth, and simulator validation.

output_examples.input: User asks how to move from simulation to quantum hardware.
output_examples.output: A migration outline covering plugin installation, backend choice, shot count, provider credentials, cost monitoring, and hardware error handling.

best_practices: Validate circuits on local simulators before using paid or limited hardware backends.
Keep provider tokens outside shared code and use secure credential storage.
Check circuit depth, shots, gradients, and device capabilities before long runs.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API tokens into prompts, notebooks, or code examples.
Do not assume simulator gradients and hardware gradients use the same method.
Do not increase circuit depth before checking initialization and measurement design.

faq.question: Does this skill run PennyLane code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users run code in their own Python environment.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it help with quantum hardware?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains backend and plugin choices, but users must provide their own accounts and credentials.

faq.question: Does it cover quantum chemistry?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes VQE, molecular Hamiltonians, UCCSD, geometry workflows, and property calculations.

faq.question: Does it support machine learning frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers PennyLane integration concepts for PyTorch, JAX, and TensorFlow.

faq.question: What should users do before hardware runs?
faq.answer: They should test locally, estimate costs, confirm backend capabilities, and protect provider credentials.
